evals.prompt: Design a real-time event notification system in Python using FastAPI, PostgreSQL (async SQLAlchemy), and Redis. Implement a layered architecture, connection pool setup, index optimization strategy in PostgreSQL, structured logging, custom domain exceptions, and pytest integration tests with Redis/PostgreSQL mocked.
evals.expected_output: A clean, production-ready backend design and module implementing Clean Architecture layers. Contains type-annotated database setup, custom exceptions, async database calls, index recommendations, and automated tests with comprehensive fixture/mock structure.
expectations: Uses Clean Architecture principles (clear separation of Domain, Application, and Infrastructure layers)
Uses async SQLAlchemy operations with connection pooling
Includes index recommendations (EXPLAIN plans) or database transaction boundaries
Implements domain-specific custom exceptions and structured logging
Includes pytest integration tests with appropriate mocking for DB and Redis

evals.prompt: Create a responsive, premium UI dashboard component in React with TypeScript. It should present server health metrics. Use custom HSL styling (no Tailwind), CSS variables, custom SVG micro-animations/transitions for charts, dynamic data simulation, responsive flexbox/grid layout, and include unit tests using React Testing Library.
evals.expected_output: A modern React component file with TypeScript interfaces, a separate styled CSS file with custom properties, smooth transitions, semantic HTML, proper SEO tags, and tests verifying component rendering and interaction.
expectations: Uses React with TypeScript and types all component props and states
Implements premium custom HSL styling and animations via CSS variables
Ensures full responsive design (mobile/desktop media queries)
Uses semantic HTML tags and incorporates SEO best practices
Includes unit tests verifying rendering of health metrics
